master_row.title: Action Functional on Trawinistic Manifolds
master_row.canonical_statement: The action functional on a Trawinistic manifold aggregates kinetic, potential, and curvature contributions into one variational object. It is the source from which the field equations and conservation structures of the next entries are derived.
master_row.formation_note: Documented in the source as a lagrangian construction relating φ, ∇, M, L, g, n.
master_row.dictionary: The action functional (S[]) for a field () is given by (S[]=_ML_T(,^T,g),d^n x), where (L_T) includes kinetic, potential, and curvature terms compatible with the TZP structure.
master_row.encyclopedia: The action functional is the master quantity from which the Trawinistic field equations are derived. Interpretive role: This functional is the center of the variational part of the canon. Once it is fixed, least-action principles, Euler--Lagrange equations, and conserved currents all become consequences rather than separate axioms.
